title: NOC 41301 – Therapists in Counselling and Related Specialized Therapies | GTR Immigration
description: Therapists in Counselling and Related Specialized Therapies under NOC 41301 are mental health and behavioural support professionals who provide counseling, psychotherapy, and specialized therapeutic services across Canada. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role plays a critical part in supporting individuals, families, and communities facing emotional, psychological, and social challenges. These professionals work in healthcare facilities, counseling centers, rehabilitation programs, schools, and private practice settings, contributing to Canada’s expanding mental health and community wellness initiatives.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group413 – Professional occupations in social and community servicesMinor Group4130 – Social and community service professionalsNOC Code41301

duties: Therapists perform some or all of the following duties:
Interview clients and prepare case histories
Assess psychological, emotional, or behavioural problems
Develop and implement counseling and intervention plans
Provide therapy, mediation, and group sessions
Liaise with community agencies and provide referrals
Prepare assessment, follow-up, and court reports
Supervise other counselors or support staff
Provide public education and professional consultation
Provide expert testimony in legal proceedings
Conduct research and publish professional materials
Collaborate with government and workplace assistance programs
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in:CounselingPsychologyMental healthTherapyRelated social service discipline
Master’s degree required for:Art therapistsMusic therapistsDance therapists
Membership with provincial or federal professional association usually required
Registration with regulatory body required in:Nova ScotiaNew Brunswick
Registration required in Ontario for psychotherapy practice
In Quebec, registration required for:Marriage and family therapistsPsychoeducatorsSexologistsCriminologists
Foreign-trained professionals must undergo credential assessment and provincial registration before practice.
salary_prospects: Therapists in Counselling and Related Specialized Therapies typically earn between:
$36,600 – $86,600 per year, depending on specialization, experience, and province.
Private practitioners and clinical supervisors may earn higher incomes.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 41301 is positive and stable due to:
Growing demand for mental health services
Government investment in addiction and rehabilitation programs
Increased focus on youth and family support
Northern and rural communities often show higher demand.
labour_market_demand: Therapists in Counselling and Related Specialized Therapies are employed in:
Increased awareness of mental health services
Growth in youth and family counseling services
Opportunities are available nationwide, particularly in Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Quebec, and Northern territories.
Stable demand in healthcare and social services
Career progression into supervisory or clinical leadership roles
Specializations such as addiction counseling, family therapy, and psychotherapy are particularly in demand.
pathways: Professional registration must be completed before practicing in regulated provinces.
